_meta.description: Persistent living knowledge for the Global Macro monitor (GMM). Carries forward asset class baselines, active tactical alerts, standing supply shock drivers, blind spot overrides, and conviction history across weekly issues.

evidence.description: Metals under sustained pressure from tariff-driven demand destruction (11.8% effective US tariff rate as of Apr 8 2026), Hormuz closure cutting Gulf metal flows, and dollar safe-haven bid. Section 232 metals tariffs restructured Apr 6 2026 with multi-rate system (50% high-content, 25% most derivatives).

IMF WEO April 2026 projects base metals prices broadly flat in 2026 reference scenario; adverse scenario sees broader commodity price destruction as demand contracts. EM demand for metals (esp. China) critical variable.

China LPR held at 3.0% (1yr) / 3.5% (5yr) for eleventh consecutive month as of Apr 20 2026; PBoC 'moderately loose' stance supports domestic metals demand but war-related oil cost surge and external uncertainty weigh on industrial activity.

evidence.description: Strait of Hormuz partially open as of Apr 17-24 2026 with ceasefire extended but fragile; Iran asserting IRGC permission requirement for all transits. Prior to conflict ~138 vessels/day transited; now 5-10/day. IEA head Birol: 'most significant energy security threat in history' — 13M bpd lost globally.

Brent crude at ~$104 as of Apr 24 2026 (down from ~$120 peak in mid-March); WTI ~$94. Oil up >55% since Iran war began Feb 28 2026. RSM GDP forecast for 2026 revised down to 1.7%. Oxford Economics threshold: oil must average $115-$125/bbl for full quarter to trigger global GDP contraction — approaching red line in Q2.

IMF WEO April 2026 reference scenario: oil prices increase 21.4% on Middle East disruption; average petroleum spot price $82/bbl for year. Adverse scenario: 80% further oil price increase from Q2 baseline. Energy inflation component in US CPI March 2026: +12.5% YoY (+21.2% gasoline MoM).

Fed held rates at 3.5-3.75% at March 18 2026 FOMC; FOMC minutes (Apr 8) show 'almost all participants' supported hold; dot plot projects single cut in 2026; 7 of 19 members see no cuts in 2026. FOMC Apr 28-29 meeting: market consensus 99.8% probability of no change (Polymarket).

CFTC: net speculative USD longs at 18th percentile (near-record short) — squeeze risk elevated. US-Germany 10Y spread 155bps — compressing.

OMFIF March 2026: one-third of respondents flag basis and FX swap market conditions as top-three funding concern.

standing_supply_shock_drivers.name: hormuz_oil_supply_disruption

structural_facts.description: Strait of Hormuz structural chokepoint: ~20M bpd normal capacity (20% of global petroleum liquids). US-Iran war began Feb 28 2026 with US-Israeli strikes. Strait effectively closed for commercial traffic since early March 2026.

windowed_events.description: US-Israeli strikes on Iran begin. Iran retaliates. Strait of Hormuz closure begins.
Iran imposes $2M 'toll' on ships transiting via Iranian territorial waters route; US/Israel vessels excluded. ~6 ships/day transiting vs 138 normal. IEA: 13M bpd supply lost globally.

Two-week US-Iran ceasefire announced. Brent falls ~10%; Iran FM declares Hormuz 'completely open' Apr 17-18. But Iran re-asserts IRGC control within hours; ships turned back; US Navy seizes Iranian vessel Apr 18. Ceasefire extended by Trump Apr 22 due to 'fractured' Tehran politics but blockade maintained.

Brent at $104.63 Apr 24; talks in Pakistan with US delegation. Iran refuses further talks. Strait at 5-10 ships/day vs 138 normal. US blockade of Iranian ports continues. IEA head Birol: 'largest energy security threat in history'.

standing_supply_shock_drivers.current_driver: Iran conflict / Hormuz restriction — partially open under IRGC control, fragile ceasefire
standing_supply_shock_drivers.current_state_note: Ceasefire extended but US blockade of Iranian ports maintained. Brent ~$104 Apr 24. Hormuz 5-10 ships/day. Resolution requires IRGC withdrawal from transit control + mine clearance.
standing_supply_shock_drivers.resolution_criteria: AIS-confirmed resumption of commercial crossings without IRGC toll/permission requirement; geopolitical premium drops below $5/bbl; US blockade lifted

structural_facts.description: US tariff regime fundamentally restructured since Liberation Day (Apr 2 2025). Current effective tariff rate: 11.8% as of Apr 8 2026 — highest since early 1940s (exc. 2025 peak). Section 232 metals (multi-rate), Section 232 autos, Section 122 (10% global, expires July), plus new 100% pharma tariffs effective Sep 29 2026.

windowed_events.description: SCOTUS strikes down IEEPA Liberation Day tariffs. Trump imposes 10% Section 122 tariffs. Effective rate falls to ~11% but remains historically extreme.
Section 232 metals tariff restructuring takes effect: multi-rate system (50% high-metal content, 25% most derivatives, 15% threshold). Effective tariff rate rises to 11.8%.

100% Section 232 pharmaceutical tariffs announced (generics/orphan drugs exempt). Effective Sep 29 2026. Yale Budget Lab: if Sec 122 expires, end-2026 rate = 9.7%; if permanent, 12.2%. China faces 31.6% effective rate.

structural_facts.description: US expanded sanctions perimeter in Iran war context. US blockade of Iranian ports in effect. Sanctions on Russian/Iranian vessels selectively lifted to free up 'dark fleet' tanker capacity for oil re-routing. SWIFT exclusions of Iranian entities expanded.

windowed_events.description: US selectively lifts sanctions on Russian and Iranian vessels to free tanker capacity for US supply chain. Net effect: dark fleet activated for non-Hormuz supply routes.

US.fiscal_posture_basis: CBO: $1.9T deficit (5.8% GDP) FY2026; debt at 101% GDP; OBBBA tax cuts adding $4.7T over 10yr; net interest >$1T threshold crossed.
US.monetary_stance_basis: Fed funds 3.5-3.75% (held Jan, Mar 2026); dot plot: 1 cut in 2026 (7/19 see no cuts); FOMC minutes: almost all support hold; Polymarket 99.8% no change Apr 29.
US.last_regime_change_note: Last rate change was Dec 2025 cut; regime now hawkish-hold from Jan 2026.

Eurozone.fiscal_posture_basis: Germany fiscal stimulus active (fiscal rule reform 2025); EU defence spending increase. Southern periphery fiscal consolidation continuing. Blind Spot 6: defence spending on imported platforms has limited EU growth multiplier.
Eurozone.monetary_stance_basis: ECB held rates at Mar 19 2026 meeting: deposit 2.00%, MRO 2.15%, MLF 2.40%. Lagarde signaled pause Apr 15 despite upside inflation risks. Morningstar: markets now price 1-2 ECB hikes in 2026 due to Iran war energy shock — terminal rate ~2.5%. Inflation forecast 2.6% in 2026 (up from 1.9%).
Eurozone.last_regime_change_note: ECB deposit rate cut to 2.00% Jun 11 2025; held since.

UK.fiscal_posture_basis: UK economy has negative output gap per IMF; IMF WEO April 2026 revised UK growth down 0.5pp to 0.8% in 2026. Chancellor maintaining fiscal rules under pressure.
UK.monetary_stance_basis: Bank of England Bank Rate 3.75% (held, next decision Apr 30 2026). BoE cut rates 6 times since Aug 2024. Current inflation 3.3%. IMF notes UK still has negative output gap constraining tightening.
UK.last_regime_change_note: BoE began cutting cycle Aug 2024; 6 cuts total through early 2026.

Japan.fiscal_posture_basis: Japan GDP rebounded to 1.3% annualized in Q4 2025 from stronger consumption/investment. Government maintaining expansionary stance; IMF WEO 2026 forecast 0.7%.
Japan.monetary_stance_basis: BoJ held at 0.75% Apr 26-27 2026 (8-1 vote; Takata dissented for hike to 1%). Rate at highest since 1995. Bloomberg Apr 8: former BoJ official says April hike 'about time to act'; market consensus however pricing no change. Quarterly Outlook Report Apr 28. Trading Economics: 1.00% by end Q2 2026.
Japan.last_regime_change_note: BoJ hiked to 0.75% at latest increase from 0.5%.

China.fiscal_posture_basis: China growth: IMF WEO 4.4% in 2026 (from 5% in 2025). Fiscal support provided (infrastructure, domestic demand stimulus). IMF: additional fiscal support provided in Q4 2025 / early 2026. Export sector strong but domestic demand weak.
China.monetary_stance_basis: PBoC: 'moderately loose' monetary policy confirmed Jan, Feb, Mar 2026 by governor Pan Gongsheng. LPR held 3.0% (1yr) / 3.5% (5yr) for 11 consecutive months (Apr 20 2026). RRR and open market operations maintain ample liquidity. War-related energy inflation reducing urgency for further rate cuts — 'wait and see' mode as of Apr 2026.
China.last_regime_change_note: PBoC reaffirmed moderately loose stance Jan 2026; LPR held; no change to peg management framework.

G10_EM_Representative.fiscal_posture_basis: IMF: EM public debt rising to 86% GDP by 2031. Fiscal consolidation required in most commodity-importing EMs. Rating agencies maintaining negative outlooks. Fragile frontier sovereigns under pressure from energy/food import bill surge.
G10_EM_Representative.monetary_stance_basis: PIIE April 2026: many EM central banks prioritizing inflation containment over growth — tracking Fed or ahead. BIS Quarterly Review March 2026: EMEs with high USD debt and shallow FX markets forced to track US rate path.
G10_EM_Representative.last_regime_change_note: Iran war onset forced widespread EM currency intervention and tightening bias shift from early 2026 easing expectations.

blind_spots.description: Q1 earnings pre-date Liberation Day impact. Pre-tariff beats may appear in early April reports. Guidance revisions for Q2 are the real signal. Do not upgrade earnings_revisions from GREEN until Q2 guidance season is complete.

blind_spots.name: Hormuz ceasefire false-positive risk
blind_spots.description: Any announced 'opening' of Hormuz should not be scored as resolved until: (a) mine clearance confirmed, (b) IRGC permission/toll regime withdrawn, (c) AIS shows >50 commercial vessels/day for 3+ consecutive days, (d) US naval blockade of Iranian ports lifted. Apr 17-18 'open' declaration immediately reversed — serves as calibration event.

Full baseline re-verification. All 8 asset class baselines re-anchored to Apr 28 2026 readings. Active tactical alerts (dollar_weaponization WARNING, treasury_market_liquidity WARNING, fx_swap_basis ELEVATED) populated with current evidence chains. Standing supply shock drivers updated with Hormuz ceasefire status. Regime classifications populated for US/Eurozone/UK/Japan/China/G10-EM. IMF WEO April 2026 integrated (global growth 3.1% reference, 4.4% inflation). Blind Spot BS8 added for Hormuz false-positive risk. challenge f-001 Turnberry fabrication noted — no Turnberry deal in this baseline. Challenger f-002 regime-delta logical inconsistency: CRISIS/Stable confirmed as valid (ceasefire extended but CRISIS conditions persist). Effective tariff rate corrected: current 11.8% (post-SCOTUS Section 122), not 22% (prior IEEPA peak).

us_fiscal_sustainability.assessment: UNSUSTAINABLE — CBO trajectory projects 120% debt/GDP by 2036 (above 1946 WWII peak). $9.8T 2026 maturity wall with higher refinancing rates creating structural interest cost expansion. OBBBA tax cuts ($4.7T/10yr) add to deficit. No credible fiscal consolidation path visible in current Congressional environment.

recession_indicators.recession_signal_note: 57% of Americans in surveys say economy in recession despite official data showing growth. Oxford Economics threshold: oil must average $115-125/bbl for full quarter for GDP contraction — approaching red line in Q2 2026 with Brent projected to average $115 through Q2. Stagflation confirmed: growth decelerating while inflation re-accelerating.

_challenge.justification: Two hard flags on fabricated Turnberry deal and regime logic, plus soft flags on blind spots and sources warrant human review before publication.

findings.quoted_claim: EU-US Turnberry tariff deal locks in 15 percent rate on EU exports reducing uncertainty but eroding EU competitiveness
findings.finding_preliminary: No evidence of any EU-US Turnberry tariff deal exists in web searches as of 2026-04-21. Turnberry refers to a Trump golf resort, not a known trade agreement, and the cited ING Think article discusses general tariff impacts without mentioning Turnberry or a specific 15 percent locked rate deal.

evidence.relevance: Article projects EU export slowdown to US at 4.6 percent in 2026 due to tariffs but does not mention Turnberry deal or locking in 15 percent rate

evidence.source: Web search for Turnberry tariff deal
evidence.relevance: No results for EU-US Turnberry tariff deal; Turnberry associated with Trump properties, not trade policy

findings.baseline_disposition: RETRACTED — no evidence of Turnberry tariff deal in any source. EU tariff rate on exports to US is 15% per Liberation Day Wikipedia table (as of Aug 1 2025) under Section 122/reciprocal regime — not a negotiated deal.

findings.finding_preliminary: Stress regime remains CRISIS with Stable delta, but guardrails require corroborating evidence across at least two domains for any non-Stable delta; here delta is Stable yet lead signal and multiple domains cite new Turnberry deal as structural entrenchment without prior brief evidence, violating conjunctivity rule implicitly by introducing unverified driver.
findings.baseline_disposition: RESOLVED — f-001 Turnberry fabrication removed. CRISIS/Stable is internally consistent: conditions are CRISIS-level but delta is Stable (no new regime-changing escalation since Apr 21). Confirmed valid.

findings.quoted_claim: earnings_revisions flag: ELEVATED, EPS revision breadth -15 percent, source: FactSet
findings.finding_preliminary: Earnings_revisions flagged ELEVATED (not GREEN), so Blind Spot 1 trigger does not apply, but Tech asset_class lacks any caveat on buyback distortion despite guardrail expectation for GREEN cases; FactSet data unverifiable for exact -15 percent without URL.
findings.baseline_disposition: CAVEAT ADDED — Blind Spot 7 (tariff-era earnings distortion) remains active; BS5 also active for Tech/Consumer Staples. -15% EPS revision breadth noted as approximate; FactSet URL not available in baseline.

findings.quoted_claim: m2_money_supply flag: ELEVATED direction: DETERIORATING value: M2 YoY growth 2.1 percent
findings.finding_preliminary: Nominal M2 at 2.1 percent YoY triggers Blind Spot 2 for Crypto (flag ELEVATED + DETERIORATING), and Crypto has banking distrust warning but lacks specific real M2 caveat as required; no T1/T2 source confirms exact 2.1 percent for week ending 2026-04-18.

evidence.source: Federal Reserve H.6 Money Stock Measures
evidence.relevance: Latest H.6 shows M2 YoY around 1.8-2.0 percent as of mid-April 2026, close but requires verification; real M2 negative per CPI adjustment

findings.baseline_disposition: CAVEAT ADDED — M2 YoY updated to 1.8-2.0% range (mid-April 2026 H.6 per challenger evidence). Real M2 confirmed negative after 3.3% CPI. Blind Spot 2 (Rule 2) confirmed active.

findings.quoted_claim: trump_tariffs current_driver: USMCA review and Section 232 enforcement
findings.finding_preliminary: Tariff_tracker cites 2026-04-02 effective dates unverifiable; prior brief mentions Section 122 and Yale estimates at 9.6-11 percent, not 17.2 percent effective rate without T1 source.

evidence.relevance: Prior reports effective tariff 11.0 percent, highest since 1943

findings.baseline_disposition: CORRECTED — Yale Budget Lab Apr 8 2026 confirmed as T1 source: effective tariff rate 11.8% as of Apr 8 2026 (highest since early 1940s exc. 2025 peak). Prior 22% figure was IEEPA-era (pre-SCOTUS strike). Wharton Penn Budget Model Apr 15: ETR 8.9% through Feb 2026 (different methodology). Both cited in tariff_escalation_protocol.

findings.quoted_claim: Energy blind_spot_warning: Energy equity vs commodity divergence blind spot active Hormuz driver unresolved
findings.finding_preliminary: Acknowledges documented false-positive 4 (energy commodity vs equity) and Hormuz-flip risk per guardrails 6.1, but prior briefs describe Hormuz closure as ongoing post-ceasefire with persistent disruption, not resolved.

evidence.relevance: Hormuz traffic far below pre-war levels post-April 8 ceasefire

findings.baseline_disposition: CONFIRMED — Blind Spot BS8 added to baseline specifically addressing Hormuz false-positive risk. Apr 17-18 'open' declaration calibration event documented. Hormuz status correctly assessed as partially open / fragile as of Apr 24.

gaps_register.finding: Spec dimension 3 (standing supply shock drivers) calls for 'tariff regimes, sanctions architecture, energy infrastructure shocks' as explicit sub-entries with structural_facts[] / windowed_events[] two-bucket discipline. Current state has oil_supply_shock_driver (non-standard field) plus tariff_escalation_protocol (non-standard field). This baseline populates standing_supply_shock_drivers[] array as a new module per spec, while preserving legacy fields for backward compatibility. Operator should confirm: (a) whether oil_supply_shock_driver and tariff_escalation_protocol should be deprecated in favor of standing_supply_shock_drivers[], or (b) maintained as parallel structures.
gaps_register.suggested_action: Operator review: consolidate legacy oil_supply_shock_driver + tariff_escalation_protocol into standing_supply_shock_drivers[] in next cycle OR explicitly document as parallel-surfacing structures.

gaps_register.finding: Spec validation contract requires _meta.baseline_method == 'dr-claude-2026-04'. This run is dispatched as 'computer-research-subagent' (not Claude directly). Sentinel field set to 'dr-computer-2026-04-28' to be accurate. Operator should confirm whether the validation gate sentinel should be updated to accept 'dr-computer-2026-04' pattern, or this run should be re-dispatched under a Claude model.
gaps_register.suggested_action: Operator decision: accept dr-computer-2026-04-28 for this sprint OR re-dispatch under Claude to satisfy strict spec validation.

gaps_register.finding: BIS Quarterly Review March 2026 on FX swap basis available (cited) but BIS Quarterly Review April 2026 (if published) not yet retrieved. FX swap basis current level in bps not sourced to a primary. Clarusft Q1 2026 volumes cited but basis spread level (e.g. EUR/USD 3m cross-currency basis in bps) not confirmed for Apr 2026.
gaps_register.suggested_action: Weekly cycle: retrieve BIS April 2026 QR when published; add FX swap basis level in bps to fx_swap_basis alert evidence.

gaps_register.finding: Challenger finding f-001 (Turnberry fabrication) in _challenge.findings was from prior publish run and is retained per consolidation rule. This baseline formally disposes f-001 as RETRACTED and f-002 through f-006 as resolved/caveated. The _challenge block from Apr 21 is preserved as historical. Future challenge will supersede.
gaps_register.suggested_action: No action required — disposition documented in baseline_disposition fields on each finding.
